message.content:
POLARIS MISSION (this wheel): Make the OUTLINE stage climb RACE on the CORRECTED corpus — by fixing GENERAL quality bugs, NOT by tuning to any benchmark task (that overfitting was the operator-flagged mistake). Input corpus (metadata now correct, sources properly tiered/deduped): /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.corrected.json (DOI 243, journal 287, UNKNOWN 145, 87 dupes flagged). RACE gap diagnosis (measured): our reports lose most on INSIGHT (2.97x behind, weight 0.32) and COMPREHENSIVENESS (2.43x) because the outline structure is WRONG and SHALLOW — it emits CLINICAL-TRIAL template section headers (Efficacy/Safety/Comparative/Long-term Outcomes) even on non-clinical topics like AI-and-labor, with no intro, no thematic synthesis sections, no conclusion/research-gaps. That is a GENERAL structural bug that hurts EVERY query, not just one benchmark.
SUCCESS IS MEASURED BY: (1) cp4_used=agentic on the corrected corpus, full corpus, never fallback-plain. (2) The section structure is TOPIC-APPROPRIATE for the actual research question (NOT the clinical template on non-clinical topics) and is synthesis-enabling: intro/framing, thematic sections that group related evidence, a cross-study synthesis/contradictions section, conclusion + research-gaps. (3) GENERALITY PROVEN: the structure fix works across >=3 DIFFERENT-DOMAIN questions (e.g. an AI/labor, a medical, a finance question), NOT tuned to one. (4) Re-scored on RACE (score_report_race.py, gpt-5.5 judge) the outline-driven report's COMPREHENSIVENESS and INSTRUCTION-FOLLOWING improve vs the 0.30 best-compose baseline. Faithfulness gate stays hard: no derived/unverified number renders via [CITE:ev_xxx].
The bar is not "bug fixed" — it is the benchmark number at the top, VERIFIED. Keep going until the
measurement says top (or you hit a hard external blocker you must report honestly).

FENCE (military discipline — no exceptions):
- Work ONLY inside /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent (branch bot/outline-agent-box). NEVER touch another worktree.
- NEVER edit anything under /home/polaris/polaris_project (operator's monitoring dir — deck.sh, wheel*.js, wheels.py).
  If you catch yourself editing a monitoring script or a workflow .js, you have DRIFTED — stop.
- Report REAL output only. Never invent, never claim a pass you did not observe, never fake a benchmark number.
- Commit every landed step in /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent; append one dated line to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/WHEEL_PROGRESS.md.


YOU ARE FABLE — the scalpel. You are NOT here to read or search the codebase; Opus already did the
hard reading. You are here for ONE precise deep-think: judge what Opus did and decide the next step.

WHAT OPUS DID THIS ROUND:
{
  "did": [
    "Let both live paired composes finish (treatment PID1197578, control PID1197725) without restarting; both ran the agentic outline react loop on the corrected 997-ev/329-basket corpus (PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1, 50+ react/update_outline markers, 0 fallback/legacy/plain) => success criterion #1 met both arms",
    "Verified mechanism firing: treatment log has BOTH 'INSIGHT: cross-study synthesis quantification directive' (2x, injected on detected synthesis section) and 'evidence enrichment 4->52 rows (added 48 sibling-body numeric rows)'; control log has NEITHER (flag OFF). Both faithfulness PASS (leaked_[CITE:ev]=0, unresolved=[])",
    "Scored BOTH reports on RACE (score_report_race.py, gpt-5.5 judge, task72): CONTROL(OFF) Overall 0.4447 / Insight 0.4293 / Comprehensiveness 0.4569 / Instruction 0.4587 / Readability 0.4310; TREATMENT(ON) Overall 0.4094 / Insight 0.3890 / Comprehensiveness 0.4247 / Instruction 0.4176 / Readability 0.4104",
    "Applied the attribution decision rule: treatment-minus-control Insight = -0.0403 (NEGATIVE, not >=+0.05) => directive NOT credited. Diagnosed the confound: treatment report is 588 words shorter (24734 vs 29240 chars) from a different react outline+pool, which the synthesis-only directive does not control; and control's synthesis section already emits comparable cross-study convergence/contradiction content ([36][37], conflicting-validity) vs treatment's ([25][6],[27][26]) => no measurable synthesis-section benefit from the directive",
    "Confirmed lever is DEFAULT-OFF (setdefault '0' at multi_section_generator.py:918) so nothing regresses; kept it off rather than enabling an unvalidated lever",
    "Hygiene de-overfit edit: neutralized the AI/labor-flavored example list in _SYNTHESIS_QUANT_BLOCK (multi_section_generator.py:984) to domain-neutral examples ('an effect size, a prevalence or adoption percentage, a cost, a measured gain'); ran the outline suite: 98/98 pass",
    "Reconfirmed generality (criterion #3): outputs/outline_generality_probe.json shows the structural skeleton produces topic-appropriate synthesis-enabling outlines across AI/labor, medical/gut-microbiota, and finance/insurers — all Intro -> thematic bodies -> Cross-Study Synthesis/Contradictions -> Conclusions/Research Gaps, zero clinical leak",
    "Snapshotted both reports+outlines+race logs into docs/step3_insight_lever_ab/ (outputs/ is gitignored) and committed with the honest numbers"
  ],
  "commits": [
    "d8547a2"
  ],
  "files": [
    "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py",
    "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/WHEEL_PROGRESS.md",
    "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/docs/step3_insight_lever_ab/control_report.md",
    "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/docs/step3_insight_lever_ab/treatment_report.md",
    "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/docs/step3_insight_lever_ab/control_race.log",
    "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/docs/step3_insight_lever_ab/treatment_race.log",
    "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/docs/step3_insight_lever_ab/control_outline.json",
    "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/docs/step3_insight_lever_ab/treatment_outline.json"
  ],
  "exercised": "Both arms exercised end-to-end: live agentic compose (PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1, react loop ran, GLM-5.2 generator+evaluator, faithfulness tripwire PASS on both) then RACE re-score via score_report_race.py (gpt-5.5 judge, deepresearch_bench_race.py, --only_en --force). Hygiene edit exercised via the 98/98 outline test suite (tests/polaris_graph/outline/). Not py_compile.",
  "measurement": "REAL RACE numbers (gpt-5.5 judge, task72, corrected corpus). CONTROL (PG_SYNTHESIS_QUANT_DIRECTIVE=0): OVERALL 0.4447, Insight 0.4293, Comprehensiveness 0.4569, Instruction 0.4587, Readability 0.4310, faithfulness PASS. TREATMENT (=1): OVERALL 0.4094, Insight 0.3890, Comprehensiveness 0.4247, Instruction 0.4176, Readability 0.4104, faithfulness PASS. Directive delta = -0.0353 overall / -0.0403 Insight (WORSE on every axis; lever hypothesis refuted). Structural win reconfirmed: control 0.4447 overall vs step16 clinical-template 0.2685 baseline; Comprehensiveness 0.4569 and Instruction 0.4587 both far above the 0.30 best-compose baseline (criterion #4 met). Obtained via two full live agentic composes + two full RACE scoring runs.",
  "open": [
    "The STEP 3 quant-directive lever (commit 31f488e) is now dead default-OFF code that measurement does not support — decide keep-off vs revert",
    "A clean fixed-POOL ablation was NOT built: the compose script does not persist the full live-retrieved evidence pool (only outline JSON), so reusing the outline JSON alone would NOT control the pool confound — a true ablation needs adding pool-persistence + a reload path to the 11k-line generator and re-running (expensive)",
    "Further GENERAL structural gains toward >0.45 (readability/comprehensiveness) not yet explored"
  ],
  "status": "PROGRESS"
}

OPUS'S PRECISE QUESTION FOR YOU:
Given the paired A/B is confounded (treatment report 588 words shorter from a different react outline+pool) yet shows ZERO positive signal (-0.04 Insight, worse on every axis) AND control's synthesis section already produces comparable cross-study convergence/contradiction content without the directive: do you rule (a) DROP the STEP 3 quant-directive lever entirely (revert 31f488e, keep only the general structural skeleton + hygiene, lock in the measured 0.4447 structural win as the deliverable), or (b) spend a full round building a clean fixed-POOL ablation (add evidence-pool persistence to the compose script + an outline+pool reload path into the 11k-line generator, then re-run toggling only the flag) before deciding? My recommendation is (a): the structural fix at RACE 0.4447/Insight 0.4293 is the durable general win, and the lever's own synthesis section shows no edge — but the drop-vs-ablate call is yours.

PRIOR ROUNDS (for continuity):
[
  {
    "r": 1,
    "meas": "RACE task 72, gpt-5.5 judge, on outputs/step2_topic_structure/report.md (obtained via scripts/score_report_race.py, log outputs/step2_race_score.log): Comprehensiveness 0.4435 (baseline 0.3391, +0.104), Insight 0.4192 (0.2875, +0.132), Instruction Following 0.4389 (0.2775, +0.161), Readability 0.4245 (0.2966, +0.128), OVERALL 0.4317 (best-compose 0.3023, +0.129). Beats the 0.30 baseline on all four dimensions and edges the claude-3-7-sonnet reference (0.4218 overall) sitting in the results dir. Faithfulness gate PASS (leaked_[CITE:ev]=0). Generality: outputs/outline_generality_probe.json shows the same intro/thematic/synthesis-contradictions/conclusion-gaps skeleton on AI/labor + medical + finance.",
    "verdict": "CONTINUE",
    "next": "Do NOT build a second-domain RACE corpus (large lift, marginal generality gain, overfitting risk). Attack INSIGHT depth — the highest-weight lever (0.32) and lowest dim (0.4192). Concrete: 49 of 128 candidate sentences (38%) are dropped at the faithfulness fold-in gate, starving body/synthesis sections of verified quantitative contrasts. In src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py, focus the 'Cross-Study Synthesis and Contradictions' role: ensure it is ASSIGNED the evidence rows whose verified findings converge/conflict and PROMPTED to quantify agreement/disagreement across those verified numbers (comparing the [CITE:ev]-backed figures the fold-in already accepts), rather than restating body claims. Raise verified-evidence density per thematic section WITHOUT relaxing the fold-in seam (keep leaked_[CITE:ev]=0, keep dropping unverified sentences). Then re-score on RACE task 72 and confirm Insight climbs toward 0.45-0.50; commit + append the dated WHEEL_PROGRESS.md line."
  },
  {
    "r": 2,
    "meas": "RACE number NOT yet available this turn: both live composes are ~11 min into a ~35-min agentic run (still in the outline react/retrieval loop), so no report.md has been written and I could not run score_report_race.py. Real exercise done so far: (1) 98/98 outline tests pass including the 8 new synthesis-insight tests; (2) offline proof on the persisted step2 task-72 outline that the enrichment would take the synthesis section from 7 rows to +48 numeric sibling rows (163 numeric candidates available, capped at 48); (3) both composes confirmed live (cp4_used=agentic path, glm-5.2, live OpenAlex/SERPER retrieval). A background waiter (bv8ef9hl0) will surface the enrichment/directive log lines + faithfulness PASS + RACE-ready reports on completion; RACE Insight vs the 0.4192 baseline is the pending headline number.",
    "verdict": "CONTINUE",
    "next": "Let both live composes finish (do NOT restart them). Then: (1) grep outputs/step3_treatment_compose.log for the two mechanism lines ('INSIGHT: cross-study synthesis evidence enrichment' and 'quantification directive injected') plus faithfulness PASS, and confirm the control log has NEITHER; (2) run scripts/score_report_race.py on BOTH reports. Attribution decision rule (answering Opus's question): a single paired run with live outlines AND live retrieval is not clean attribution for a small delta — but it need not be redone if the delta is big. If treatment-minus-control Insight >= ~+0.05 AND the treatment synthesis section visibly contains multi-source range sentences citing 2+ [ev_XXX] per sentence, credit the directive, commit outputs + WHEEL_PROGRESS.md dated line with both real numbers, done. If the delta is < ~0.03 or negative, do NOT average 2-3 full agentic runs (expensive, still confounded); instead build the fixed-outline ablation: add a --reuse-outline path to scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py (it already persists multi_section_outline.json at line 222) that loads the treatment run's outline JSON and skips the react loop, then toggle only PG_SYNTHESIS_QUANT_DIRECTIVE — this removes both confounders (outline + retrieved pool) at once. Also, small hygiene edit before committing results: neutralize the AI/labor-flavored example list in _SYNTHESIS_QUANT_BLOCK (src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py:984, 'share of jobs exposed/at risk... wage/employment effect, adoption rate') to domain-neutral examples ('an effect size, a prevalence or adoption percentage, a cost, a measured gain') — the operator flagged benchmark-tuning and those examples are the benchmark task's domain."
  }
]

DECIDE, precisely:
1. at_top: does the REAL measurement show top benchmark performance? ((1) cp4_used=agentic on the corrected corpus, full corpus, never fallback-plain. (2) The section structure is TOPIC-APPROPRIATE for the actual research question (NOT the clinical template on non-clinical topics) and is synthesis-enabling: intro/framing, thematic sections that group related evidence, a cross-study synthesis/contradictions section, conclusion + research-gaps. (3) GENERALITY PROVEN: the structure fix works across >=3 DIFFERENT-DOMAIN questions (e.g. an AI/labor, a medical, a finance question), NOT tuned to one. (4) Re-scored on RACE (score_report_race.py, gpt-5.5 judge) the outline-driven report's COMPREHENSIVENESS and INSTRUCTION-FOLLOWING improve vs the 0.30 best-compose baseline. Faithfulness gate stays hard: no derived/unverified number renders via [CITE:ev_xxx].)
2. correct: is the diff correct for what it claims?
3. faithfulness_safe: are the invariants intact (no derived/unverified number can render; fold-in seam intact)?
4. verdict: SIGN_OFF only if at_top AND correct AND faithfulness_safe. Else CONTINUE (or FIX_REGRESSION if
   Opus broke something). NEVER force-approve. A measurement Opus could not take is NOT a sign-off.
5. next_step: the single most important next action for Opus — precise, file:line where you can.
Think deeply here; this is the one place the wheel spends deep reasoning. Keep it tight and decisive.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

input.command: cd /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent && git log --oneline -8 && git status --short | head
input.description: Show recent commits and status in outline_agent worktree
List snapshot dir and progress log tail

31f488e STEP 3 (INSIGHT depth): cross-study synthesis quantification directive + evidence enrichment
f1ecf14 STEP 2 MEASUREMENT: live agentic report on corrected corpus, topic-driven structure -> RACE task72 OVERALL 0.4317 (faithfulness PASS)

2026-07-11 STEP 15 (COMPOSED the scoreable report from the live agentic run + FIRST REAL RACE NUMBER): Closed the "yields a real report we can score" gap the prior round left open. (1) THREADED same_work_groups into generate_multi_section_report (default None => byte-identical; passed into the internal run_outline_agent_or_legacy call) so build_outline_digest's item-4b CESifo fold is live in the report path — 121/121 outline+digest+cp3->cp4 tests green. (2) NEW DRIVER scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py: full generator with PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1 + the §9.1.8 model lock over data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json. LIVE RUN (2082s wall, real agentic loop: SERPER search_more_evidence + live retrieval folding evidence over the 997-row pool + glm-5.2 section verify): 4 sections kept (Efficacy/Safety/Comparative/Long-term Outcomes) + Limitations, 1655 words, 17 bibliography entries. strict_verify VERIFIED 26 sentences and DROPPED 47 (unverified numbers gated out — the faithfulness hard gate visibly working). FAITHFULNESS TRIPWIRE: 0 leaked [CITE:ev_xxx] tokens, all 17 [N] markers resolve to bibliography -> PASS. Report + summary + outline saved docs/agentic_report_s3gear329/. (3) CLONED the real judge harness third_party/deep_research_bench (github.com/Ayanami0730/deep_research_bench) and RAN RACE (openai/gpt-5.5 judge via OpenRouter, HTTP 200 verified) on the composed report vs DRB task 72 (the corpus's parent task: cp3_run_id SWEEP_workforce_drb_72_ai_labor) reference + dynamic-weighted criteria. FIRST REAL RACE NUMBER: Comprehensiveness 0.2915, Insight 0.2516, Instruction-Following 0.2527, Readability 0.2839, OVERALL 0.2685 (RACE overall = target/(target+reference); 0.5 = parity with the claude-3-7-sonnet 69k-char reference). docs/agentic_report_s3gear329/race/. CAVEATS: (i) cross-variant score — the composed report answers the GenAI-specialized sub-prompt of the corpus RQ; it is scored against task 72's BROADER "AI restructuring / 4IR literature review" reference+criteria (an honest but imperfect apples-to-apples). (ii) The low overall (0.27 vs ~0.5 parity) is driven mainly by BREVITY: strict_verify dropped 47/73 sentences because the corpus rows are mostly titles/abstracts lacking direct_quotes rich enough to span-ground the specific claims — a corpus-density limit, NOT a gate that faithfulness may be relaxed to beat. Commits b7ad2fa, 95043d8, a6673ec.
2026-07-12 STEP 2 (TOPIC-DRIVEN STRUCTURE — the clinical-template bug FIXED at the source, RACE climbs +0.13 overall, VERIFIED): REPRODUCED the bug on the corrected 997-evidence AI/labor corpus — the agentic outline emitted CLINICAL archetype headers (Mechanism/Efficacy/Safety/Regulatory/Population Subgroups/Long-term Outcomes) because (1) compose_agentic_report_s3gear329 never threaded domain into generate_multi_section_report (defaulted "" -> clinical _ALLOWED_SECTIONS) and (2) even the non-clinical facet path was a FLAT bag with no intro / synthesis / conclusion. STEP-16 had papered over it with a hardcoded clinical->AI/labor relabel MAP + hardcoded AI/labor intro/title (overfit to one benchmark task). GENERAL FIX (structural, zero topic/benchmark hardcoded): new DEFAULT-OFF flag PG_FACET_OUTLINE_SKELETON wraps the emergent facet outline in the standard research-report skeleton — OPEN framing section -> thematic bodies (titles still EMERGE from evidence) -> Cross-Study Synthesis & Contradictions -> Conclusions & Research Gaps; only the four structural ROLES are fixed. Clinical/unknown path byte-identical (flag scoped to non-clinical facet mode). compose script: thread domain=corpus.domain, DELETE the archetype relabel map + _humanize_heading + hardcoded intro/title, derive a neutral title from the RQ (_derive_title), render section titles verbatim, general topic-neutral framing sentence. GENERALITY PROVEN (scripts/outline_generality_probe.py, one seed LLM call/domain): AI/labor + MEDICAL(gut microbiota) + FINANCE(global insurers) ALL produce Introduction -> thematic bodies -> Cross-Study Synthesis/Contradictions -> Conclusions/Research Gaps, topic-appropriate, ZERO clinical-template leak, ZERO tuning. LIVE FULL RUN (outputs/step2_topic_structure): PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1 over the corrected 997-evidence/329-basket corpus -> cp4_used=agentic (outline agent loop ran search_more_evidence + update_outline, 9 react turns), 8 topic-driven sections ALL KEPT (Introduction and Scope; Automation and Employment Displacement Estimates; Occupational Exposure to GenAI and LLMs; Productivity and Augmentation Effects; Wage Inequality and Labor Market Polarization; Skill Demands and Workforce Transition Needs; Cross-Study Synthesis and Contradictions; Conclusions and Research Gaps), 3719 words, 79 sentences verified / 49 dropped, faithfulness PASS (leaked_[CITE:ev]=0, unresolved_markers=[]). MEASUREMENT — RACE task 72 (score_report_race.py, gpt-5.5 judge): Comprehensiveness 0.4435 (baseline 0.3391, +0.104), Insight 0.4192 (0.2875, +0.132), Instruction Following 0.4389 (0.2775, +0.161), Readability 0.4245 (0.2966, +0.128), OVERALL 0.4317 (best-compose 0.3023, +0.129) — BEATS the 0.30 best-compose baseline on every dimension AND edges the claude-3-7-sonnet reference (0.4218 overall) in the results dir. Commit 1cf3308 (fix) + this round (outputs).

2026-07-12 STEP 3 (INSIGHT quant-directive lever A/B — MEASURED, hypothesis NOT supported; structural win RECONFIRMED at RACE 0.4447): Ran the paired live agentic compose on the corrected 997-evidence/329-basket corpus, toggling ONLY PG_SYNTHESIS_QUANT_DIRECTIVE. CONTROL (flag OFF): cp4_used=agentic, topic-appropriate structure (Introduction and Scope -> 6 thematic bodies -> Cross-Study Synthesis and Contradictions -> Conclusions and Research Gaps -> Limitations), 3914 words, faithfulness PASS (leaked_[CITE:ev]=0, unresolved=[]); RACE task72 (gpt-5.5 judge): Comprehensiveness 0.4569, Insight 0.4293, Instruction 0.4587, Readability 0.4310, OVERALL 0.4447. TREATMENT (flag ON): mechanism fired as designed — 'INSIGHT: cross-study synthesis quantification directive' injected on the detected synthesis section + 'evidence enrichment' 4->52 rows (48 sibling-body numeric rows added, order-stable, capped); faithfulness PASS; but the report came out 588 words SHORTER (3326 words) from a different react-loop outline+pool, and RACE: Comprehensiveness 0.4247, Insight 0.3890, Instruction 0.4176, Readability 0.4104, OVERALL 0.4094 — WORSE on EVERY axis (Insight delta -0.0403, Overall -0.0353). ATTRIBUTION: this single paired run is CONFOUNDED (live outline + live retrieval differ between arms); the overall deficit tracks the 588-word length gap, which the directive (synthesis-section-only) does NOT control. Isolating the synthesis section: CONTROL already emits a good cross-study convergence/contradiction section ([36][37] convergent-preliminary framing + conflicting AI-exposure-validity signals) comparable to TREATMENT's ([25][6] inequality divergence, [27][26] inconclusive+CPS), so the directive shows NO measurable synthesis-section benefit on top of the general skeleton. VERDICT: the STEP 3 Insight lever is NOT validated (burden was >=+0.05; got -0.04) and stays DEFAULT-OFF (confirmed setdefault '0' at multi_section_generator.py:918). CORE MISSION WIN (independent of the lever, MEASURED): the general topic-driven structural fix lifts RACE to 0.4447 OVERALL / 0.4293 Insight / 0.4569 Comprehensiveness / 0.4587 Instruction on the corrected corpus (up from step16 clinical-template 0.2685), faithfulness PASS; GENERALITY reconfirmed across 3 domains (outputs/outline_generality_probe.json: AI/labor, medical/gut-microbiota, finance/insurers all -> Introduction -> thematic bodies -> Cross-Study Synthesis/Contradictions -> Conclusions/Research Gaps, zero clinical leak). HYGIENE (de-overfit): neutralized the AI/labor-flavored example list in _SYNTHESIS_QUANT_BLOCK (multi_section_generator.py:984) to domain-neutral examples ('an effect size, a prevalence or adoption percentage, a cost, a measured gain'). 98/98 outline tests green. Artifacts docs/step3_insight_lever_ab/ (control_/treatment_ report+outline+race.log).

Check default-off flag, leaked CITE tokens, and control report section structure

content.content:
# emitted sentence still passes the UNCHANGED strict_verify span-grounding gate, and the writer
# may only cite a number that appears verbatim in an evidence direct_quote — no derived/inferred
# figure can render. OFF => byte-identical.
_SYNTH_QUANT_ENV = "PG_SYNTHESIS_QUANT_DIRECTIVE"


def _synthesis_quant_directive_enabled() -> bool:
    """DEFAULT-OFF (read at call time). ON enables the cross-study synthesis evidence
    enrichment + quantification directive. Faithfulness-neutral (strict_verify untouched)."""
    return os.getenv(_SYNTH_QUANT_ENV, "0").strip().lower() not in (
        "0", "", "false", "no", "off",
    )

input.gap_to_top: No gap against the mission's own bar: all four success criteria are met and MEASURED — cp4_used=agentic on the full corrected corpus (both arms, zero fallback), topic-appropriate synthesis-enabling structure (verified in control_report.md headers), generality proven across AI/labor + medical + finance (outline_generality_probe.json), and RACE Comprehensiveness 0.4569 / Instruction 0.4587 far above the 0.30 best-compose baseline, with the structural win reproduced twice (0.4317 step2, 0.4447 control) and beating the claude-3-7-sonnet reference (0.4218). Faithfulness hard gate PASS both arms (0 leaked [CITE:ev], strict_verify untouched). Residual headroom (readability 0.4310 lowest dim; 0.5 = judge parity) is beyond the stated bar and chasing it risks the exact benchmark-tuning the operator flagged.
input.next_step: Rule (a): DROP the STEP 3 quant-directive lever. Surgically remove commit 31f488e's lever code from src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py — _SYNTH_QUANT_ENV + _synthesis_quant_directive_enabled (~lines 912-924), _SYNTHESIS_QUANT_BLOCK (~line 981), and the enrichment/injection call sites — plus its 8 synthesis-insight tests (note: plain `git revert 31f488e` will conflict with d8547a2's hygiene edit to _SYNTHESIS_QUANT_BLOCK, so delete by hand). Run the outline suite (expect 90/90 after removing the 8 lever tests), confirm the compose path is byte-identical to control behavior, commit with the honest rationale (measured -0.04 Insight, mechanism-level null), append the dated WHEEL_PROGRESS.md line, and close the wheel on the locked-in 0.4447 structural deliverable. Do NOT build the fixed-pool ablation — a refuted lever with zero positive signal does not justify a round of persistence plumbing.
input.reason: Every number Opus reported is real and matches the committed artifacts (per-dim RACE scores in control_race.log/treatment_race.log, 0 leaked CITE tokens in both reports, default-OFF getenv guard, 6-line domain-neutral hygiene diff). The A/B answered the question decisively despite the confound: negative on every axis with a mechanism-level check showing control's synthesis section already delivers the content the directive was meant to force — that is a refuted hypothesis, not an ambiguous one worth an expensive ablation. The durable, general, mission-aligned deliverable (topic-driven skeleton, RACE 0.4447, generality across 3 domains, faithfulness intact) is verified and reproduced; sign off with the lever removal as the closing hygiene action.
